Source_Species.reason: The study is in tomato and names SlGRAS9 as a tomato gene; plant background is Solanum lycopersicum.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experimental materials state transgenic plants were generated in Micro-Tom (Solanum lycopersicum) background.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental procedures specify Slgras9-CR lines were generated in the Micro-Tom background.

Applied_Species.reason: All functional assays were performed in tomato plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experimental materials identify the experimental tomato as Solanum lycopersicum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Transgenic and edited lines used for SlGRAS9 assays were in the Micro-Tom background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results repeatedly report increased 'volatile terpenes/terpenoids' in Slgras9-CR tissues measured by GC-MS.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper explicitly discusses volatile terpenes/terpenoids as the affected metabolite class.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Type VI glandular trichomes are described as the main producers of terpenoids, and increases were shown in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits where type VI trichomes increased.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links glandular trichome-derived terpenoids to defense against spider mites and aphids and shows enhanced tolerance in Slgras9-CR plants.

Regulator_Type.reason: SlGRAS9 is explicitly described as a transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SlGRAS9 is one of the GRAS TFs and discussed as a GRAS transcription factor.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state SlGRAS9 is highly expressed in type VI glandular trichomes and trichome-rich tissues, highest in sepals, and is higher in trichome-poor than trichome-rich accessions.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper reports high transcript in trichomes, especially in sepals, and notes trichomes account for most expression in stems and fruits; this supports high expression in trichomes from leaves, stems, fruits, and sepals.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Results directly show SlGRAS9 binds the SlMYC1 promoter and represses SlMYC1 transcription.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Dual-luciferase, EMSA, and yeast one-hybrid support direct repression of SlMYC1; increased TPS transcripts in Slgras9-CR plus failure to regulate TPS promoters directly support indirect effects on TPS expression.

Regulation_Mode.reason: The paper explicitly states SlGRAS9 negatively regulates/represses SlMYC1 transcription.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Firefly luciferase complementation assays failed to show interaction between SlGRAS9 and Wo, Hair, SlCycB2, or SlMYC1.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: These proteins were specifically tested in luciferase complementation assays and no interaction was detected.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported in Results/Methods: CRISPR knockout, SEM trichome counts, GC-MS, RT-qPCR, dual-luciferase, EMSA, yeast one-hybrid, double mutant analysis, and pest bioassays.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The data support that SlGRAS9 represses SlMYC1, thereby negatively affecting type VI trichome formation and terpenoid accumulation; control of TPS expression is indirect.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Spider mite and aphid infestation assays were performed; greenhouse conditions are listed in Methods.

Omics_Data.reason: Results cite prior transcriptomic profiling and prior DAP-seq evidence; no accession number is provided in this paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Slgras9-CR increased type VI glandular trichomes and elevated volatile terpenoids in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits, and increased tolerance to spider mites and aphids.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Results and discussion place SlGRAS9 upstream of SlMYC1 as a negative regulator of the module controlling type VI trichomes and terpenoid accumulation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports a slight reduction of long trichomes (types I/II/III) in fruits and sepals of Slgras9-CR plants and does not report other penalties.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The summary and discussion explicitly propose SlGRAS9 as a target for breeding strategies to enhance pest resistance.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is directly supported by Results sections and figures for SlGRAS9.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion states SlGRAS9 is the tomato homolog of Arabidopsis SCL8, and Results show no direct regulation of tested TPS promoters in dual-luciferase assays.

Source_Species.reason: SlMYC1 is a tomato gene tested in tomato.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments are in Solanum lycopersicum; transgenic lines were in tomato.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state overexpression and knockout lines of SlMYC1 were in the Micro-Tom background.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses of SlMYC1 were performed in tomato plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The tomato background is Solanum lycopersicum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods explicitly state SlMYC1 overexpression and knockout lines were in the Micro-Tom background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: GC-MS analyses showed changes in terpenoid accumulation in SlMYC1-OE and Slmyc1-CR plants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The affected compounds are volatile terpenoids/terpenes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Type VI glandular trichomes are presented as the source of terpenoids, and effects were shown in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links altered terpenoid/glandular trichome status in SlMYC1 lines with spider mite and aphid tolerance.

Regulator_Type.reason: SlMYC1 is explicitly described as a transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Discussion states SlMYC1 is a basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) transcription factor.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results show SlMYC1 transcript is higher in high-trichome-density accessions and increased in young leaves and fruit trichomes of Slgras9-CR plants.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper clearly measures SlMYC1 in young leaves and fruit trichomes and across cultivars, but does not directly provide a full expression map stating broad expression in all 'trichome-rich tissues'.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The overexpression lines are explicitly denoted '35S::SlMYC1', indicating CaMV 35S promoter use.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Results directly show SlGRAS9 binds the SlMYC1 promoter and represses its transcription.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: This paper supports that SlMYC1 positively influences TPS gene expression/functionally, but the specific list 'SlTPS1; SlTPS5; SlTPS12; SlTPS9; SlTPS20' is drawn from previous studies discussed rather than directly tested as targets here.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The discussion cites prior evidence that SlMYC1 activates SlTPS1/5/12 and notes regulation is indirect; this paper's data are consistent with positive regulation of TPS expression via trichome development.

Regulation_Mode.reason: SlMYC1 is presented as a positive regulator/activator of TPS-related output and type VI trichome formation.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The statement about forming a module with Wo and recruitment to TPS promoters by Wo is explicitly from previous work, not experimentally demonstrated in this paper; only the downstream-of-SlGRAS9 part is supported here.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Wo as an interactor is mentioned only from prior studies in the Discussion, not validated in this paper.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results include overexpression, CRISPR knockout, SEM trichome counts, GC-MS, genetic interaction with Slgras9-CR, pest bioassays, and RT-qPCR.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: This paper directly supports positive regulation of type VI glandular trichome development and associated terpenoid biosynthesis by SlMYC1.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Spider mite and aphid infestation assays plus greenhouse growth conditions are described.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show overexpression increases type VI trichomes and terpenoid accumulation, while knockout reduces type VI trichomes and terpenoids to almost undetectable levels.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper presents SlMYC1 as essential for type VI trichome differentiation and strongly affecting terpenoid accumulation across tissues.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show Slmyc1-CR shifts trichome composition toward type VII and VII-like trichomes and the pest assays show reduced tolerance; no overexpression penalty is reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Summary and discussion propose up-regulation/overexpression of SlMYC1 as a target to improve pest resistance.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All five summarized points match the Results for SlMYC1 overexpression, knockout, epistasis with Slgras9, terpenoids, and pest tolerance.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This paper indeed positions SlMYC1 downstream of SlGRAS9, and direct binding to TPS promoters is not established here; prior work cited indicates indirect activation through Wo.
